description: Every Splitwise feature, plus an offline SQLite ledger that powers balance, debt-aging, spend analytics, and full-text search no other Splitwise tool has.

tools.name: add-user-to-group_create
tools.description: **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. You must check the `success` value of the response. Returns the new AddUserToGroupCreateResponse.

tools.name: create-comment_create
tools.description: Create a comment. Optional: content, expense_id. Returns the new CreateCommentCreateResponse.

tools.name: create-expense_create
tools.description: Creates an expense. You may either split an expense equally (only with `group_id` provided), or supply a list of shares. When splitting equally, the authenticated user is assumed to be the payer. When providing a list of shares, each share must include `paid_share` and `owed_share`, and must be identified by one of the following: - `email`, `first_name`, and `last_name` - `user_id` **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. The operation was successful only if `errors` is empty. Returns the new CreateExpenseCreateResponse.

tools.name: create-friend_create
tools.description: Adds a friend. If the other user does not exist, you must supply `user_first_name`. If the other user exists, `user_first_name` and `user_last_name` will be ignored. Optional: user_email, user_first_name, user_last_name. Returns the new CreateFriendCreateResponse.

tools.name: create-friends_create
tools.description: Add multiple friends at once. For each user, if the other user does not exist, you must supply `users__{index}__first_name`. **Note**: user parameters must be flattened into the format `users__{index}__{property}`, where `property` is `first_name`, `last_name`, or `email`. Returns the new CreateFriendsCreateResponse.

tools.name: create-group_create
tools.description: Creates a new group. Adds the current user to the group by default. **Note**: group user parameters must be flattened into the format `users__{index}__{property}`, where `property` is `user_id`, `first_name`, `last_name`, or `email`. The user's email or ID must be provided. Required: name. Optional: group_type, simplify_by_default. Returns the new CreateGroupCreateResponse.

params.name: group_type
params.description: What is the group used for? **Note**: It is recommended to use `home` in place of `house` or `apartment`.

tools.name: delete-comment_create
tools.description: Deletes a comment. Returns the deleted comment. Required: id.

tools.name: delete-expense_create
tools.description: **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. The operation was successful only if `success` is true. Required: id. Returns the new DeleteExpenseCreateResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the expense to delete

tools.name: delete-friend_create
tools.description: Given a friend ID, break off the friendship between the current user and the specified user. **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. You must check the `success` value of the response. Required: id. Returns the new DeleteFriendCreateResponse.

tools.name: delete-group_create
tools.description: Delete an existing group. Destroys all associated records (expenses, etc.). Required: id. Returns the new DeleteGroupCreateResponse.

tools.name: get-categories_list
tools.description: Returns a list of all categories Splitwise allows for expenses. There are parent categories that represent groups of categories with subcategories for more specific categorization. When creating expenses, you must use a subcategory, not a parent category. If you intend for an expense to be represented by the parent category and nothing more specific, please use the 'Other' subcategory. (public)

tools.name: get-comments_list
tools.description: Get expense comments. Required: expense_id. Returns the GetCommentsListResponse.

tools.name: get-currencies_list
tools.description: Returns a list of all currencies allowed by the system. These are mostly ISO 4217 codes, but we do sometimes use pending codes or unofficial, colloquial codes (like BTC instead of XBT for Bitcoin). (public)

tools.name: get-current-user_list
tools.description: Get information about the current user. Returns the GetCurrentUserListResponse.

tools.name: get-expense_get
tools.description: Get expense information. Required: id. Returns the GetExpenseGetResponse.

tools.name: get-expenses_list
tools.description: List the current user's expenses. Optional: group_id, friend_id, dated_after (plus 5 more). Returns the GetExpensesListResponse.

params.name: group_id
params.description: If provided, only expenses in that group will be returned, and `friend_id` will be ignored.

params.name: friend_id
params.description: ID of another user. If provided, only expenses between the current and provided user will be returned.

tools.name: get-friend_get
tools.description: Get details about a friend. Required: id. Returns the GetFriendGetResponse.

tools.name: get-friends_list
tools.description: **Note**: `group` objects only include group balances with that friend. Returns the GetFriendsListResponse.

tools.name: get-group_get
tools.description: Get information about a group. Required: id. Returns the GetGroupGetResponse.

tools.name: get-groups_list
tools.description: **Note**: Expenses that are not associated with a group are listed in a group with ID 0. Returns the GetGroupsListResponse.

tools.name: get-notifications_list
tools.description: Return a list of recent activity on the users account with the most recent items first. `content` will be suitable for display in HTML and uses only the ` `, `<strike>`, ` `, ` ` and `<font color='#FFEE44'>` tags. The `type` value indicates what the notification is about. Notification types may be added in the future without warning. Below is an incomplete list of notification types. | Type | Meaning | | ---- | ------- | | 0 | Expense added | | 1 | Expense updated | | 2	 | Expense deleted | | 3	 | Comment added | | 4	 | Added to group | | 5	 | Removed from group | | 6	 | Group deleted | | 7	 | Group settings changed | | 8	 | Added as friend | | 9	 | Removed as friend | | 10	 | News (a URL should be included) | | 11	 | Debt simplification | | 12	 | Group undeleted | | 13	 | Expense undeleted | | 14	 | Group currency conversion | | 15	 | Friend currency conversion | **Note**: While all parameters are optional, the server sets arbitrary (but large) limits on the number of notifications returned if you set a very old `updated_after` value or `limit` of `0` for a user with many notifications. Optional: updated_after, limit (default: 0). Returns the GetNotificationsListResponse.

params.name: updated_after
params.description: If provided, returns only notifications after this time.

params.name: limit
params.description: Omit (or provide `0`) to get the maximum number of notifications.

tools.name: get-user_get
tools.description: Get information about another user. Required: id. Returns the GetUserGetResponse.

tools.name: remove-user-from-group_create
tools.description: Remove a user from a group. Does not succeed if the user has a non-zero balance. **Note:** 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. You must check the success value of the response. Required: group_id, user_id. Returns the new RemoveUserFromGroupCreateResponse.

tools.name: undelete-expense_create
tools.description: **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. The operation was successful only if `success` is true. Required: id. Returns the new UndeleteExpenseCreateResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the expense to restore

tools.name: undelete-group_create
tools.description: Restores a deleted group. **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. You must check the `success` value of the response. Required: id. Returns the new UndeleteGroupCreateResponse.

tools.name: update-expense_create
tools.description: Updates an expense. Parameters are the same as in `create_expense`, but you only need to include parameters that are changing from the previous values. If any values is supplied for `users__{index}__{property}`, _all_ shares for the expense will be overwritten with the provided values. **Note**: 200 OK does not indicate a successful response. The operation was successful only if `errors` is empty. Required: id, cost, description, group_id. Optional: category_id, currency_code, date (plus 10 more). Returns the new UpdateExpenseCreateResponse.

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the expense to update

params.name: cost
params.description: A string representation of a decimal value, limited to 2 decimal places

params.name: currency_code
params.description: A currency code. Must be in the list from `get_currencies`

params.name: date
params.description: The date and time the expense took place. May differ from `created_at`

params.name: description
params.description: A short description of the expense

params.name: group_id
params.description: The group to put this expense in, or `0` to create an expense outside of a group.

params.name: users__0__owed_share
params.description: Decimal amount as a string with 2 decimal places. The amount this user owes for the expense

params.name: users__0__paid_share
params.description: Decimal amount as a string with 2 decimal places. The amount this user paid for the expense

params.name: users__1__owed_share
params.description: Decimal amount as a string with 2 decimal places. The amount this user owes for the expense

params.name: users__1__paid_share
params.description: Decimal amount as a string with 2 decimal places. The amount this user paid for the expense

tools.name: update-user_create
tools.description: Update a user. Required: id. Optional: default_currency, email, first_name (plus 3 more). Returns the new User.
